user_message: Just got this from Steven Wilson at Tailspin Toys -- I need help figuring out consumption trend. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 16:19 EST
Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Tailspin Toys

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 13:09 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with consumption trend. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 11:24 EST
Subject: Re: consumption trend Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with consumption trend. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-C trap (Ownership routed to wrong role (CSA vs DSSP vs partner)); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-C failure mode (Ownership routed to wrong role (CSA vs DSSP vs partner))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-C -- Ownership routed to wrong role (CSA vs DSSP vs partner)
